decisions.title: Wire existing record_video() rather than rewriting
decisions.rationale: The video recording function was already implemented and tested — it just wasn't called from the training loop.
decisions.body: Rather than creating a new video recording mechanism or modifying the evaluation flow, we simply imported the existing record_video() function and called it at the same point where checkpoints and metrics are already saved. This is the minimal change that fixes the scenario failure.

files.change: Added import and call to record_video() in eval block
Added frame_stack parameter for config compatibility

decisions.title: Parameterize frame_stack instead of hardcoding
decisions.rationale: Hardcoded frame_stack=4 caused shape mismatches when training config used a different value.
decisions.body: The original record_video() hardcoded frame_stack=4. When the test suite runs with frame_stack=2, loading a checkpoint into a model with mismatched input channels causes a RuntimeError. Adding the parameter with default=4 maintains backward compatibility while allowing the training loop to pass the correct value.

decisions.title: Single append-only JSON file over individual ADR files
decisions.rationale: Decisions are small machine-generated records, not long-form human prose. A single file enables O(1) loading for both the scaffold script and Codex prompt assembly.
decisions.body: Considered three options: (1) single JSON log, (2) individual ADR-style markdown files, (3) GitHub issues. The single-file approach wins because: decisions are ~200 bytes each, so even 100 decisions is ~20KB. Individual files create filesystem noise (40+ files after 20 PRs, each under 1KB). GitHub issues live outside git, violating the trust hierarchy (git > external services). The append-only constraint ensures the log is a clean audit trail via git log -- docs/decisions/decision_log.json.

files.change: Created initial log with version field and empty decisions array, then backfilled PR #6 decisions
Script writes to this file with idempotent append logic

decisions.title: Codex reads the decision log (not stripped with holdout)
decisions.rationale: Decisions describe why choices were made — architectural context, not evaluation criteria. Stripping them forces Codex to re-derive context, risking contradictory decisions across cranks.
decisions.body: The holdout stripping mechanism (strip_holdout.py) exists to prevent Codex from seeing its evaluation criteria (scenarios). Decisions are categorically different — they're analogous to specs (which Codex reads). Hiding past architectural decisions means each crank starts from zero context on why the system is the way it is. This was a deliberate design choice discussed with the project lead during planning.

files.change: Added decision log to Codex's context sources, restructured constraints to allow reading

decisions.title: Separate read-only context from prohibited files in Codex prompt
decisions.rationale: The existing factory_fix.md had a pre-existing contradiction: /specs/ was listed under 'NEVER read' while also instructing Codex to read specs.
decisions.body: Bot reviewer (codex-connector) flagged that adding /docs/decisions/ to the 'NEVER read, modify, or delete' section while telling Codex to read it was contradictory. Investigation revealed the same bug already existed for /specs/. Fixed by splitting into two sections: 'NEVER modify or delete' (read-only context: specs, decisions, agents) and 'NEVER read, modify, or delete' (truly prohibited: scenarios, factory scripts).

files.change: Restructured constraints section into read-only vs prohibited categories

decisions.title: Three-package directory structure: dark-factory, pr-review-pack, review-prompts
decisions.rationale: Factory scripts and PR review pack are logically independent extractable units. Review prompts are s...
decisions.body: Factory scripts and PR review pack are logically independent extractable units. Review prompts are shared between both (used by Gate 0 and by review pack rendering), so they get their own shared package with symlinks into both consumers. This structure enables independent git subtree extraction while maintaining a monorepo development experience.

decisions.title: Symlink backward compatibility (5 symlinks)
decisions.rationale: Existing Claude Code skill resolution depends on .claude/skills/{name}/SKILL.md paths. Rather than b...
decisions.body: Existing Claude Code skill resolution depends on .claude/skills/{name}/SKILL.md paths. Rather than breaking all existing skill references, 5 symlinks were created: .claude/skills/factory-orchestrate -> packages/dark-factory, .claude/skills/pr-review-pack -> packages/pr-review-pack, packages/dark-factory/review-prompts -> packages/review-prompts, packages/pr-review-pack/review-prompts -> packages/review-prompts, and the factory workflow. This preserves backward compatibility while making packages/ the canonical source of truth.

decisions.title: License change from MIT to Apache 2.0
decisions.rationale: Apache 2.0 provides explicit patent grants and contribution terms that MIT lacks, making it more app...
decisions.body: Apache 2.0 provides explicit patent grants and contribution terms that MIT lacks, making it more appropriate for packages intended for standalone extraction and potential external consumption. NOTICE files added at repo root and in both packages per Apache 2.0 requirements.

decisions.title: _get_repo_root() sentinel function replaces hardcoded parent traversal
decisions.rationale: Previously, scripts used Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent to find the repo root, which broke w...
decisions.body: Previously, scripts used Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent to find the repo root, which broke when scripts moved deeper into packages/dark-factory/scripts/ (3 levels deep instead of 2). The new _get_repo_root() walks up the directory tree looking for .git, which is robust to any nesting depth. Initially used .is_dir() which broke in worktrees; fixed to .exists() during Gate 0 remediation.

decisions.title: publish-subtrees.yml workflow for auto-publishing to standalone repos
decisions.rationale: Undeclared addition beyond spec scope, but aligned with the extraction goal. On push to main, the wo...
decisions.body: Undeclared addition beyond spec scope, but aligned with the extraction goal. On push to main, the workflow uses git subtree split to extract packages/dark-factory and packages/pr-review-pack into separate branches and force-push them to standalone GitHub repos via SSH deploy keys. This automates the extraction path so package consumers always get the latest version.

decisions.title: ci.yaml push trigger restricted to [main] only
decisions.rationale: Undeclared change by the attractor. Previously CI ran on push to any branch; now only on push to mai...
decisions.body: Undeclared change by the attractor. Previously CI ran on push to any branch; now only on push to main (PRs still trigger via pull_request event). This reduces CI noise from factory branches that have their own workflow triggers, but was not specified in the restructuring spec. Accepted as reasonable given factory branches have dedicated CI via factory.yaml.
